title: Charred Taco Casserole
description: A twist on traditional tacos, this casserole is a hearty and flavorful dish perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add cumin and chili powder; cook for 1 minute.
Add charred corn to the skillet; cook for 2-3 minutes.
In a separate bowl, combine shredded cheese and a pinch of salt and pepper.
In a 9x13 inch baking dish, arrange 2 tortillas to cover the bottom.
Spread half of the corn mixture over the tortillas, followed by half of the cheese mixture.
Repeat the layers, starting with the tortillas, then the corn mixture, and finally the cheese mixture.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5-10 minutes before serving.
